title: Wirelessly powered display and system
abstract: A wirelessly powered display comprises a substrate,an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate, an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate, and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate. The electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna, the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power, and the electronic circuit includes a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. A wirelessly coupled display system includes a device including a wireless power transmitter that provides wireless power within a specified range and one or more wirelessly powered displays located within the range and responsive to the wireless power provided by the device to display an image.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A hybrid high-security document, comprising: a document; and one or more independent light-emitting modules disposed on or embedded in the document, each module comprising: an antenna with multiple turns, an electronic circuit, and a light emitter mounted and electrically connected on a substrate separate from the document, wherein the electronic circuit is responsive to electrical power provided from the antenna to control the light emitter to emit light. 2. The hybrid high-security document of claim 1, wherein the electronic circuit is formed in a micro-transfer printed integrated circuit having a fractured tether, wherein the light emitter is formed in a micro-transfer printed semiconductor having a fractured tether, or wherein the light-emitting module is a micro-transfer printed module having a fractured tether. 3. The hybrid high-security document according to either claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the electronic circuit is formed in a micro-transfer printed integrated circuit or semiconductor that has an area less than one hundred thousand, fifty thousand, forty thousand, twenty-five thousand, ten thousand, five thousand, two thousand, or one thousand, five hundred, two hundred fifty, or one hundred square microns. 4. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the light-emitting module has an area less than one hundred thousand, fifty thousand, forty thousand, twenty-five thousand, ten thousand, five thousand, two thousand, or one thousand, five hundred, two hundred fifty, or one hundred square microns. 5. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 4, comprising a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 6. The hybrid high-security document according to claim 5, wherein the power converter is a micro-transfer printed power converter having a fractured tether. 7. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 6, comprising an acoustic wave filter, a surface acoustic wave filter, or a bulk acoustic wave filter. 8. The hybrid high-security document according to claim 7, wherein the acoustic wave filter has a single dominant acoustic resonant filter mode. 9. The hybrid high-security document according to either claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the acoustic wave filter is a micro-transfer printed acoustic wave filter having a fractured tether. 10. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 9, comprising a flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread incorporated in, laminated to, or woven into the document and wherein the one or more independent light-emitting modules are mounted on, embedded in, or micro-transfer printed onto the flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread. 11. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread includes paper, plastic, impregnated paper, or metal foil or wherein the flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread is electrically insulating. 12. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread is electrically insulating. 13. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 12, comprising a plurality of light-emitting modules. 14. The hybrid high-security document according to claim 13, wherein the light-emitting modules of the plurality of light-emitting modules are electrically independent and electrically disconnected. 15. The hybrid high-security document according to either claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the light-emitting modules of the plurality of light-emitting modules are disposed to form at least one of a character, a graphic indicator, an icon, a number, a letter, and a pictogram or indicates a value, a date, or a person. 16. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the document has visible markings and the one or more light-emitting modules are disposed in a location corresponding to a portion of the visible markings to highlight or otherwise indicate or emphasize the portion of the visible markings. 17. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the electronic circuit controls the light emitter within a light-emitting module to flash once or to flash sequentially. 18. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the one or more light-emitting modules include inorganic light-emitting diodes that emit different colors of light. 19. The hybrid high-security document according to claim 18, wherein the different colors of light include red, green, and blue. 20. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the electronic circuit is responsive to power received by the antenna to cause the light emitter to emit light or to send an electromagnetic signal. 21. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the antenna is a multi-layer antenna formed in multiple layers. 22. The hybrid high-security document according to claim 21, wherein the multi-layer antenna includes an even number of layers. 23. The hybrid high-security document according to either claim 21 or claim 22, wherein at least a portion of the antenna is located between the electronic circuit and the document. 24. The hybrid high-security document according to either claim 21 or claim 22, wherein the electronic circuit is between at least a portion of the antenna and the document. 25. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 21 to 24, wherein the antenna layers are alternately connected near the edge of the antenna and near the center of the antenna. 26. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 21 to 25, wherein the electronic circuit is in a layer and the antenna layer farthest from the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the electronic circuit layer nearer to the center of the electronic circuit layer than to the edge of the electronic circuit layer. 27. The hybrid high-security document according to any one of claims 1 to 26, wherein the electronic circuit and light emitter are powered solely by the energy received from the antenna. 28. A hybrid high-security document mat, comprising: a mat circuit; and an antenna, wherein the mat circuit provides a continuous or pulsed NFC signal, the pulsed NFC signal having a pulse rate of ten, twenty, fifty, or one hundred pulses per second or greater. 29. The hybrid high-security document mat according to claim 28, comprising a display responsive to the mat circuit. 30. A multi-element light-emitting system, comprising: a plurality of independent light-emitting modules, each independent light-emitting module comprising: an antenna with multiple turns, an electronic circuit, and 

 a light emitter mounted and electrically connected on a separate substrate, the independent light-emitting modules disposed in a pattern to form a visible indicator. 31. A method of making a hybrid high-security document, comprising: providing a document having visible markings; providing a source having a plurality of printable light-emitting modules; and disposing one or more of the light-emitting modules onto the hybrid document or onto a flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread subsequently incorporated in, laminated to, or woven into the document. 32. The method according to claim 31, comprising micro-transfer printing the light-emitting modules from a source substrate onto the document. 33. The method according to claim 31, comprising micro-transfer printing the light-emitting modules from a source substrate onto a flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread subsequently incorporated in, laminated to, or woven into the document. 34. The method according to any one of claims 31 to 33, comprising micro-transfer printing one or more light emitters and an integrated circuit onto a substrate to form a light-emitting module and the light-emitting modules incorporate the substrate. 35. The method according to any one of claims 31 to 34, comprising micro-transfer printing an acoustic wave filter onto the substrate to form the light-emitting module. 36. The method according to claim 34, comprising micro-transfer printing the light-emitting modules from the substrate onto the document or onto a flexible substrate, ribbon, film, or thread subsequently incorporated in, laminated to, or woven into the document. 37. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 36, comprising micro-transfer printing a plurality of the light-emitting modules in a single step and wherein the substrate has an area 

 or dimension that is equal to or larger than a corresponding area or dimension of the documents or flexible substrates. 38. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 37, wherein the substrate has an extent or area that is within a range of one tenth to ten times an extent or area of the flexible substrate, within a range of one quarter to four times an extent or area of the flexible substrate, within a range of one half to two times an extent or area of the flexible substrate, or within 25%, 10%, or 5% of an extent or area of the flexible substrate. 39. A method of using a hybrid high-security document, comprising: providing a hybrid high-security document according to claim 1; and exposing the hybrid high-security document to an electromagnetic field so that the antenna provides power to the electronic circuit and causes the light emitter to emit light. 40. The method according to claim 39, comprising observing the light or detecting the light with a light detector. 41. The method according to claim 39, wherein the electronic circuit stores information, and comprising providing the electromagnetic field, reading the information, and displaying the information on a display or transferring the information to a computer, computer system, or network-connected computer or computer server. 42. The method according to any one of claims 39 to 41, wherein the electronic circuit stores information, and comprising providing the electromagnetic field and writing information into the electronic circuit. 43. The method according to claim 42, comprising storing the information in an information registry independent of the hybrid high-security document. 44. A wirelessly powered display, comprising: a substrate; an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate; 

 an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate; and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein : the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna; the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power; and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 45. The wirelessly powered display according to claim 44, wherein the display excludes a battery, internal power source, or wired power source and the display operates solely on the electrical power provided through the antenna. 46. The wirelessly powered display according to either claim 44 or claim 45, wherein the display has a single pixel. 47. The wirelessly powered display according to either claim 44 or claim 45, wherein the display has multiple pixels. 48. The wirelessly powered display according to any one of claims 44 to 47, wherein the display is a sticker. 49. The wirelessly powered display according to any one of claims 44 to 48, wherein the displayed image is a fixed image. 50. The wirelessly powered display according to any one of claims 44 to 49, wherein the display comprises an acoustic wave filter, a surface acoustic wave filter, or a bulk acoustic wave filter. 51. A wirelessly coupled display system, comprising: 

 a device comprising a wireless power transmitter that provides wireless power within a specified range; and one or more wirelessly powered displays located within the range and responsive to the wireless power provided by the device to display an image, wherein each wirelessly powered display comprises: a substrate; an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate; an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate; and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein: the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna; the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power; and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 52. The wirelessly coupled display system according to claim 51, wherein the device is a portable device, the device is a mobile device, the display is in a fixed location, or the display is mobile or affixed to a mobile device or portable device. 53. The wirelessly powered display according to either claim 51 or claim 52, wherein the display is mounted on the device. 54. The wirelessly powered display according to either claim 51 or claim 52, wherein the one or more displays are physically separate from the device. 55. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 54, wherein the wireless power transmitter is a near-field communication device. 56. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 55, wherein the device is a mobile telephone or a smart phone. 57. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 56, wherein: the device comprises a stored image and an image transmitter circuit that wirelessly provides the stored image to the display; and the display is responsive to the wirelessly provided image to display the image. 58. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 57, wherein the display comprises a display controller. 59. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 58, wherein the device comprises a primary device display and the display is a secondary display mounted on or incorporated into the device. 60. The wirelessly coupled display system according to any one of claims 51 to 59, wherein: each display has an identification and a circuit for responding to the identification when the identification is transmitted; and the device comprises a transmitter circuit that wirelessly provides one or more of the identifications to the one or more displays. 61. A wirelessly powered display, comprising: a substrate; and a plurality of light-emitting modules disposed on the substrate, each light-emitting module having an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate, an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate, and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light- emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein, for each light-emitting module, the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna, the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power, and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 62. A wirelessly coupled display system, comprising: a device comprising a wireless power transmitter that provides wireless power within a specified range; and a wirelessly powered display located within the range and responsive to the wireless power provided by the device to display an image, wherein the wirelessly powered display comprises: a substrate; and a plurality of light-emitting modules disposed on the substrate, each light- emitting module having an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate, an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate, and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein, for each light- emitting module, the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna, the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power, and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 63. A wirelessly powered display, comprising: a plurality of substrates, each substrate having an antenna with multiple turns disposed on the substrate, an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate, and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein on each substrate the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna, the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power, and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current. 64. A wirelessly coupled display system, comprising: a device comprising a wireless power transmitter that provides wireless power within a specified range; and one or more wirelessly powered displays located within the range and responsive to the wireless power provided by the device to display an image, wherein each wirelessly powered display comprises a plurality of substrates, each substrate having an antenna with multiple turns 

 disposed on the substrate, an electronic circuit disposed on the substrate, and one or more pixels each having one or more inorganic light-emitting diodes disposed on the substrate; wherein on each substrate the electronic circuit is electrically connected to the one or more inorganic LEDs and the antenna, the antenna is responsive to electromagnetic energy to provide electrical power, and the electronic circuit comprises a power converter that converts a signal with a relatively high current and low voltage to a signal with a relatively high voltage and low current.
